author: Shadow6six using AI
install_msg:
MusicLinker has been installed!

It now automatically detects Spotify track and YouTube music/video links in messages and can reply with cross-platform search links (YouTube ↔ Spotify ↔ Tidal ↔ Amazon Music) + Brave lyrics search.

**Default: disabled per server**

Enable and configure with:
`[p]ml`  or  `[p]musiclinker`    ← shows settings
`[p]ml toggle`               ← enable/disable
`[p]ml channel #music`       ← restrict to a channel
`[p]ml react`                ← toggle reaction mode (🎵)

For better Spotify metadata (artist + album), set API credentials:
`[p]ml spotifyapi <client_id> <client_secret>`
(Recommended to run this in DMs for privacy)
name: MusicLinker
short: Converts music links between platforms + lyrics search
description:
Detects Spotify track links and YouTube (including music.youtube.com) links in messages.

Features:
• Cross-platform search links: YouTube / YouTube Music ↔ Spotify ↔ Tidal ↔ Amazon Music
• Brave Search lyrics link
• Two modes: auto-reply or 🎵 reaction mode (any user can click to reveal links)
• Channel whitelist / all channels option
• Optional Spotify Web API integration for full artist, album & track name data
• Thumbnail display toggle
• Max embeds per message limit

Disabled by default per server — must be explicitly enabled.
end_user_data_statement: This cog does not persistently store any end-user data. It processes message content in real-time to detect music links and only stores server-specific settings (enabled state, channel restriction, reaction mode, etc.) and globally-stored Spotify API credentials (if set by the bot owner).
